capability: structural-reveal discrimination — isolate a line whose bond joins two previously-separate regions of the entity graph
claim: A bond between two STANDING entities sitting in separate graph components is a structural reveal; the interior connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it and ~0 on surface-matched controls (adjacent re-bond, fresh entity), while the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (ranks fresh-mass lines above it); the connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
stimulus_shape: text · discrimination+invariance · L3 · whole-reading · target CON
verdict: CONFIRMED — controls dark (adj=0, fresh=0), bridge unique-argmax on the reveal (1.0), bayes argmax elsewhere (gap real), surface-invariant (unmarked==marked)
mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph: a bond's endpoints in different components -> 1, same-component geodesic delta -> (delta-1)/Dinf, adjacent -> 0; endpoints resolved through the CAUSAL SYN-merge identity quotient (the engine's own union-find, restricted to sentIdx<=cursor) so a reveal that names a standing entity by a new surface form is seen
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/bridge.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN channel readingAt({bridge:true}) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the mass one-surprise (surprise.js) has no atom for connectivity — a bond among standing entities is a tiny mass deposit; the structural convergence leaves no trace in the figure field. The existing diagnostic (scripts/reveal-discrimination.mjs bridgeSurprise) located this but lived only in the read-only harness AND keyed on raw surface ids, so it MISSED short-name reveals and faked a 2-hop separation on adjacent controls.
parity: npm test 649->658 (9-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (bridge opt-in)
generalization: 194/194 lines of the original reveal-stimulus.json reproduce the prior raw-id bridge column EXACTLY (no regression); coref-resolution is a strict superset — identical where reveals use full names, strictly better on short-name reveals
omnimodal: CONFIRMED on a pure-operator log (no text/parser): cross-component bond -> 1.0, adjacent -> 0, SYN-coref reveal -> 1.0 — the mechanism reads only operators, so any organ emitting CON/SIG/SYN inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ emits bonds (CON/SIG) and identity merges (SYN); structural reveals that join graph regions; reveals naming a standing entity by a new surface form
scope_fails: a reveal carried by NO bond (a pure predication/contradiction leaves connectivity unchanged — the enacted significance loop's province); convergence the weak SVO parser does not draw as a bond or shared object (front-end limit, not the channel's)

capability: the novelty reserve as a SIGNAL-DERIVED amplitude — the reserved mass for an unseen atom tracks the gamma-decayed RATE of recent first-appearances instead of the hand-rolled constant NOVELTY_RESERVE
claim: a rate-derived reserve (nu = Sum_{f<at} gamma^(at-1-f) over first-appearance steps) improves predictive surprise where novelty is positively autocorrelated (bursts) and regresses it where novelty is anti-correlated (maximally-spaced newcomers); the same mechanism cuts both ways because it tracks the RATE of newcomers but is blind to their AUTOCORRELATION
stimulus_shape: text · dissociation · L2 prediction · whole-reading · two novelty regimes (burst/drought vs spaced)
verdict: CONFIRMED DISSOCIATION, NOT PROMOTED — stream-01 (positively autocorrelated) deltaSurprisal -0.2026 (helps), stream-02 (anti-correlated) deltaSurprisal +0.2433 (regresses); sign-split as predicted; ships OPT-IN behind opts.signalReserve, default path byte-identical (676/676 green)
mechanism: noveltyAmplitude(firstSeen, at, gamma) in src/core/surprise.js, fed as novelty into surpriseAt + forwardDist over the proposition field and into the figure-field Z/pNovel in reading.js; the same helper threads through src/surfer/sequence.js so the n-gram reader inherits it (one interior, two senses); cold-start falls back to the SEED NOVELTY_RESERVE; opening guards make a zero reserve well-defined (no name-snow, no NaN)
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new export noveltyAmplitude from src/core/surprise.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js and src/surfer/sequence.js as an OPT-IN channel (opts.signalReserve) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the constant reserve is a fixed prior over the unseen; its share novelty/(sumPrior+novelty) shrinks only as accumulated mass grows, blind to how FAST genuine newcomers arrive. A signal-derived reserve sees the rate but not the autocorrelation, so it mis-weights anti-correlated streams (a long recurrence run leaves the reserve thin right before a newcomer, and a recent newcomer wrongly inflates it before a recurrence) — the deeper gap an autocorrelation-aware reserve must close
phylogeny: consolidates a population of ~10 parallel variant PRs all proposing the same evolution: #90/92/93/97/98/104 (consensus gamma-decayed first-appearance amplitude), #95/#103 (equivalent streaming recurrence decayed=gamma*decayed+newcomers), #94 (distance-list form with explicit SEED cold-start fallback — adopted), #91 (OUTLIER: a different theory — bornNoveltyReserve = gamma-weighted FRACTION of recent steps with any newcomer, clamped to [alpha,1-alpha], wired only to the forward channel; bundled an unrelated cycle-002 adjunct/referent parser fix, left out as a separate future experiment). #93 contributed the surfer sequence.js second-sense wiring; #95/#97 the opening guards; #104 the deterministic opts.signalReserve boolean gate; #98 the central scientific finding (rate is not enough — needs autocorrelation structure), which is this experiment's verdict of record
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 676/676 green; default readingAt + predictiveSequenceReading byte-identical (opts.signalReserve opt-in); lock added: tests/novelty-reserve.test.js
generalization: the dissociation is structural, not lexical: it holds on any one-figure-per-line stream and reproduces on the surfer sequence reader (a second front-end) — the reserve lives in the shared core, so any organ that calls surpriseAt/forwardDist/foldBefore inherits both the gain and the failure mode
omnimodal: the helper reads only first-appearance STEPS and gamma — no text, no parser — so any modality whose front-end emits a basis (tonal moves, frequency bins, cells) inherits the same rate-derived reserve and the same autocorrelation blind spot
scope_holds: signals with positively-autocorrelated or sustained novelty (bursty casts, a stream of fresh figures); any organ emitting an open basis through the core
scope_fails: anti-correlated novelty (regresses); a blanket DEFAULT (breaks byte-identical parity AND the net corpus sign is unknowable to a rate-only reserve) — hence NOT PROMOTED

capability: the omnimodal reading COUNT is signal-derived, not universal — readingCount reads how many readings a field holds as a void (deriveNull, log scale) over its eigengap spectrum, generating readings at a real elbow and dissipating to one reading (abstaining) on a flat spectrum
claim: the Born rule (assign each unit to argmax |<u|lens>|^2) is the UNIVERSAL cross-modal invariant; the segmentation COUNT is not. A geography-derived count beats both hand-rolled fixed-count rules — 90%-Born-mass (over-segments: saturates cap-12, flickers) and eigenvalues-above-the-void-floor (over-abstains on flat high-rank spectra) — on aggregate boundary F1 across a multilingual + hard battery, AND abstains exactly (zero boundaries) on every flat-geography stream
stimulus_shape: multimodal (11 scripts + 8 hard problems) · dissociation+abstention · reading-count · whole-stream · target COUNT
verdict: CONFIRMED — mean boundary F1 GEO 38% vs null 31% vs mass 20%, wins/ties 11/20, both margins clear key.min_margin (0.03); abstention exact on all 6 flat streams (noise/single/collinear/zh/ja/hi); Born assignment identical across all three readers
mechanism: readingCount(eigenvalues): leading eigenvalue gaps -> largest gap is the elbow -> kept iff it clears deriveNull over the gaps on a LOG scale (gap-spacings are heavy-tailed order statistics, voidnull's percolation setting). Cleared -> k=max(2,elbow) (a cleared gap separates >=2 reading groups); not cleared -> k=1, abstain. Born assignment to the top-k eigenlenses then segments by median-smoothed assignment switching. The SAME test both counts and abstains.
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new PURE export readingCount from src/core/voidnull.js beside deriveNull, re-exported from src/core/index.js; NOT wired into any default reader (opt-in count an omnimodal organ calls in place of a hand-set K)
root_cause_of_gap: a universal K is a number you invent: 90%-mass tracks total Born mass (blind to the elbow -> saturates the cap on any spread spectrum -> flicker), and eigenvalues-above-the-void tests each eigenvalue against the noise floor (a flat high-rank spectrum of many near-equal readings falls below it -> false abstain). The elbow is the geography both miss: the void applied to the GAP spectrum finds where a top group parts from the tail, and reads flatness as one reading.
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 1761->1767 (6-test lock tests/omnimodal-reading-count.test.js added); readingCount is pure (no module state, no input mutation); all 1756 prior tests byte-identical
generalization: the finding is cross-lingual by construction: it holds on 8 scripts a tokenizer never sees (character-trigram front-end) and on 8 synthetic hard problems; readingCount reads only an eigenvalue array, so any organ emitting a density inherits it
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — readingCount takes a bare Born spectrum (no text, no parser): the seed/ four-sense demo (text/audio/vision/IMU) and all 20 battery streams feed the same operator; the Born assignment on top is modality-blind
scope_holds: any field whose eigengap spectrum has a resolvable elbow above its heavy-tailed gap background; any modality emitting a density; abstention on flat spectra
scope_fails: near-degenerate multiplicity (two near-equal readings) and adjacent-recurrence boundaries (A|A) are invisible to lens-switching by construction; bases too flat to separate the true readings (CJK/Devanagari char-trigrams) -> honest abstention, a FRONT-END limit the count cannot repair
